So usually it's a hybrid as well. So I always look at the sales and I also just as much always look at the marketing side of the equation. I say, and how are they going about getting their leads? So number one is I want to see how they actually sound. Forget about what they're saying. I want to see how they sound. Do they sound good? Do they have good tonality? If they're out in the field, are they using the right body language? A lot of times there's a lot of companies, for instance, I've done transfer, for example, Wyndham, right, big vacation rental company, right? Vacation sales, right? They don't have salesmen that go out into the field, but they have people that come to them, right? So what they'll do is they have all these advertisements and free giveaways will give you this weekend here a two days free account. As long as you listen to this presentation, right? And then they'll have 50, 100 people a day come into their offices, right? And they have salesmen and then try to close them on a vacation package, right? A vacation sale, right? So in that case, you have face to face sales, right? But in their office, right? And then I can listen to them that way. But there's all these different variables, right? But I want to see how, number one, how do the salespeople sound, right? Number two, what are they actually saying? What are they saying? What words are they saying? Here's the biggest, here's one major takeaway here today for everybody is that the big mistake many of you might be making, hopefully if you're, if you've been here for a while, you're probably not doing this anymore. But the biggest mistake I see with scripts is that they, they'll, people will use scripts, but they're not actually usable scripts. The scripts are flawed in so many ways. And the two big flaws that I see are that they're not actually end to end. They, although their definition of a script will be one language pattern, one, one good way of saying something, like the first sentence of the introduction, like that's their script. You know, yeah, here's my script. And they'll give you like, it's like this long or this long. And then there's nothing after there's a looping patterns. There's no, you get it. There's no questions. They don't have that question scripted out for their intelligence gathering. So they think they're using a script, but they're not using, that's not using the script. Okay. Using a script means you have everything well thought out, the perfect words written in, in, in the spoken words. So they're actually usable, a flow like butter, you get it. So very often they're not using scripts. I want to see what they're saying. Okay. And then number three, okay, I want to see how they're handling objections. What do they do when the customers inevitably say, let me think about it, let me call you back. How are they handling all the people who sit on the fence as the phrase goes? Let me tell you something. Let me think about it. Let me call you back. Bad time here. Your success is going to be based on turning those people into customers. It's not the lay downs are the lay downs. You get it? But at the end of the day, what comes down to money is how do you take the people who say, let me think about what's their strategy for that? And then number four is what type of referral or affiliate programs do they have in place? How do they take their existence? This is by one of the biggest misses of most companies is that they don't go to their customers they have and get them to buy more. The most obvious thing of all is to go to the people that you're serving right now, that you're giving value to who you're happy customers and coming up with other products that they can use, they need that will benefit them and making money. It's a huge miss for most companies. They don't exploit that at all in a good way, right? That's one thing. So I look at the sales from sort of, you know, stem to stern, so to speak, right from top to bottom, right? And just as much as that, I also look at the marketing. Where are they getting their leads from? And are they supplying their sales force with leads? Or are they making it the job of the sales person to go out and get their own leads? I will tell you, I've had a company that I consulted for a couple hundred salesmen a year and turning over 90% of them. Only 10% would stay. I'm like, what the fuck, right? And they hired me to reduce that attrition rate. And of course, it's very easy to reduce attrition through motivation each day and also by teaching people to sound good, know what to say, how to, all the sales straight line aspect. But do you know that the, one of the, just as important as that, one of the biggest things, they were not providing their sales people with any leads. So what happened was the sales force was spending like most 80% of their day trying to even figure out who to call. You get it? Once they supply their sales force with a steady stream of leads, not qualified leads, unqualified, just leads, people to call the numbers skyrocketed. They skyrocketed. Now at the same time, of course, I also worked on their skills. I spent an awful lot of time teaching them the straight line, the sound rate to know what to say, how to handle. But I would say even more importantly, seriously, because it was just so drastic here, they were getting no leads. So they were just, they were making like 20 calls a day versus 200 calls a day. So there's a domino effect. Remember this when you're, whether you're in sales or sales manager, own the company doesn't matter. If you're not supplying your sales force with qualified leads or even unqualified leads, right? And leaving it up to them, you're making a big error in my judgment, big error, because here's the deal. Very few people who are good at sales are also great at marketing or even have that prowess. They can be taught, but they just don't know it in tune. They don't know where to go. Many salesmen are broke when they first get something. They can't afford to buy leads. The best investment you can make in yourself as a salesman or in your company as an owner or as a sales manager in your sales force, right? Is to support. But I've been through that, you know, right. And no, don't want to be dead. Who else did you get that? Okay. So now that being said, now let's I talked about this, this overarching syntax, right. And again, by the way, guys, you know, my ability to write with my right hand is going to dump. I have to get my shoulder replaced. I'll be right on my left. It probably won't be much different because I suck writing right. Yeah, right. So it's not like the straight line created those elements. Those are naturally occurring elements in any situation of influence. They must those five quailments. So I call them the five core elements of the straight line system. I'll repeat them. Number one, they must love your product. Number two, they must trust and connect with you, the salesperson definition of action threshold is it is essentially it's the level of collective certainty that any individual prospect needs to be at before they say yes. In other words, how certain does a person have to be about the product about you and the company before they naturally are inclined to say, yeah, I'll do it. I have a low action threshold, meaning yeah, I could be reasonably certain. And I'm sure I'll do it. Yeah, I'll buy it. Why not? I like to buy shit, right? My father, the opposite, he's like, I'm not buying anything unless I'm damn fucking certain, right? I was like, trust the salesman, no. So my father's got a high action threshold. I have a low one, right? Get a lower someone's action threshold if it's too high, because what someone has a high action threshold, what they say is, let me think about it. That's what they that's why the that's why the objections pop up typically, right? So it's got a very high action threshold, and they're just not certain enough yet, right? And that last element of five is your pain threshold that essentially people tend to buy quickly when they're feeling pain. And I mean pain like a pitch pain, meaning emotional pain, anguish, uncertainty, fear, they're like, they're out of control about something, they want to get back in control. That's what you do when you sell you put them back in control of their lives by giving them a product that solves a pain point. Okay. So those I call them the five core elements of the straight line system. But here's the deal. Whether you're using the straight line or not, they still exist. Right. So what happens is if you're not using the straight line, and you're unaware of them, it takes you many calls until you accidentally line them up. That's the difference. That's why we can collapse time with the straight line, because we're deliberately going after these five core elements in a methodical way, in a way that the best closes in the world do it naturally. So essentially what's a straight line represents them. I hear orphans, oh my God, from great sales. And I guess that's exactly what I do. You just wrote down what I do. Yeah, exactly. The system is represents what great salesmen do naturally and allows anyone to do that. So let's go through these steps one by one. Okay. And we'll give you the broader overview today of these steps will dig into them each week. Okay. Number one, always the first step in every sale is what you must, and everyone should know this by now, you must take immediate control. You must take control of the sale. Why? Because without control, you can't line up anything. You get it there and control there. Because if you want to do things in a predictable fashion, let me give you back the words that actually preceded me in venting the straight line what hit me is I looked at my 12 guys and don't you guys get it? Every sale is the same. They're like, what? I'm like, every sales the same watch it's a straight line. And I drew the straight line on the book. What I meant by that was that I as a world class natural salesman, I line up these same elements every time the same way because I'm in control. So I take control of the sale. I take immediate control. Okay, I'm not going to explain even how to do it today, because most of you already know we'll reinforce it next week or the week after. But the point is this is about just brought over views right now, right? You take immediate control by, you know, very quickly by sounding sharp as attack, enthusiastic as hell, an expert in your field. And that last one, an expert in your field. This is the linchpin because people we've been taught since we a be to defer to experts when we are in the process presence of someone we perceive to be an expert deferred we give up control. Okay, so you must take control why because that allows you now to line up the elements in a predictable fashion the same way every time. The second step, and this is important is we use this control, not the Baba, Baba, but to become a talking head. The mistake will make us Oh, this is when you take control, you want to talk talk talk. No, you want to use that to ask questions, we gather intelligence. We asked a certain set of questions to find out not just if our prospect needs our product, but if they get a photo product, what their beliefs are, what their values are, what their hierarchy values is, what their pain point is, the main pain point, the secondary pain points, we identify all their major important data that decide a whether they should go down the strailing can we help them and be are they qualified and where that pain lies right and then as we do that, we simultaneously get into massive rapport right on both a conscious and unconscious level. We build rapport a conscious and unconscious level by engaging in a technique called active listening and through the use of tonality when we ask all questions, we use certain tonalities that show that number one, you care and number two, you're just like them that you understand them, you use process things the same way you feel their pain. Okay, the certain tonalities that we use that allow us to fall into tight rapport. And then we also have our most importantly active listening meeting that when we ask a question and someone responds, we're not like well like uh oh uh huh mm mm mm yep god we give them the uh oh yes yeah uh oh so you do you converse with them as their uh huh yep god makes sense that you finish their sentences sometimes you know what they're gonna say that shows you're an expert but most importantly builds massive rapport and when you're done with this sort of double headed monster of gathering intelligence and building rapport, next step is your straight line transition. This is where you essentially you decide whether or not the person should continue down the straight line. Can you help them? If the answer is yes, you say well Jim based on what you said to me, this is a perfect fit for you. Let me tell you why. If not, you say Jim you know honestly I really can't help you, it's not what I have here, it's not really, it's not gonna help you out, I wish you you know great luck, maybe you can go send them somewhere else but don't try to sell someone who you can't help, it's not ethical, you're wasting your time or if they're not interested just don't don't force the square peg down there into the round hell okay but assuming they are interested then next the next step of the syntax is we make our straight line presentation and this is where we begin the process by the way of lining up those first three core elements that they must love the product trust and connect with you and trust the company that stands behind the product and again we frame we don't front load we frame okay because we're gonna end we always end our presentation with a few things so step six is we're gonna ask for the order for the first time we ask for the order now on top of that I want to add a couple things and number one remember whenever possibly want to end with urgency before we ask for the order and remember your overarching equation of energy in benefits out meaning that when you frame this all in your old training is going to the library I'm not gonna repeat it today right just very quickly when you frame a closing scenario it's very simple to get started and this you're gonna get this benefit remember reset your benefits and you ask for the order and you shut up right that this step six here closes out the front half of the sale that's it front half of the sales closed right now three doesn't work people can only take so much people don't go from imagine the certainty scale right here's a one here's a 10 this is absolute certainty they love your problem this is where they think is that you essentially untrained salesmen at best by the way they think let me I think a great presentation and I just gotta pressure someone to buy that's not what happens you frame your presentation and you keep represent presenting representing representing and each time you tell them more and so they keep saying let me think I'm gonna call you back you get them closer and closer to the so here you're gonna loop once right and you run this loop and again we're gonna dig into this in the following so I'm not gonna get specific here right every loop always ends with you asking for the order each time you ask for the order they can do what they could say yes no or maybe well again no is pretty much adding the equation so it's like yes or another objection if they say yes you have a closed sale and you're done if they say another objection what you do very simply is you're gonna loop back again so every time they hit your objection you're looping back and representing strengthening your case making them more and more certain you get it on both a logical and an emotional there's two types of certainty hopefully you know that by now if you don't that's another subject we'll deal with in the following weeks to come right but remember you keep looping back and looping back now in terms of the actual syntax watch after this first loop on the second so you get to keep running every loop ends with you asking for the order if they don't say yes they give you another objection you're gonna run another loop except this time you're gonna lower their action threshold there's a pattern that we use I'm not gonna get into it right now because it's in the old trains and I'll dig into it next week that's probably what I'll do next week right is you're you lower their action threshold and again you're gonna always end the loop with asking for the order then next loop number 10 is gonna be you're gonna add on pain and again this is a pain as the part of that's a matter of how many loops you want to run separate issue okay the point is now we get to step 11 which is right so we're gonna pay how many loops that you ran right where either you close the deal or you end the call they didn't buy it there's a certain number of loops right now I'll get into the subject of callbacks and if you want to set up a callback separate issue here we're going to go to the syntax here and if you close you is we end the call in by view but if they bought now you end go to customers for life strategies this is where you're gonna add on your strategies now for getting referrals for getting repeat buys from the same customer so there's a whole new set of strategies that kick in and step 12 if they buy now we go into phase two of the straight line which is developing customers for life how do you get them to buy from you again and again and again turn a one-time buyer into a long-term raving fan who gives you a massive number of referrals that's the syntax of the straight line eyes what the syntax allows you to do is essentially bring order to what most people in sales they see is absolute chaos they don't know what comes first what comes second emotional certainty sure so remember logical certainty is based on the words that you say in other words where you you know say my my product does this it's a cost benefit ratio it's better than the competition here's what a cost here the benefits that you have very arithmetic when someone is logically certain they can connect all the dots from front to back and say yep this makes fucking sense the product is awesome the tv sets the best tv set out there the picture quality is amazing it's easy to use it looks great on my wall you know it's got a three-year warranty um and the brand is impeccable it's the third i get the companies are great samsung is a great company or lg right but that's that's logical certainty you know it looks great seems great great price does everything it's all the smart features i want it's gorgeous to look at and emotional certainty is i can imagine how great i'll feel when the family and i are sitting around on a sunday watching a fucking movie and bonding and building memories for a lifetime how great's that gonna be it's gonna be an object for my family and i had to create wonderful memories around this tv do you see the difference in other words one of them is and imagine myself in the future using the product and enjoying it and feeling good i imagine my friend's coming over and saying wow how beautiful does that look what an awesome you know it's good it's got a great brand and imagine how good i'm gonna feel driving this with the top down alone the pacific coast highway with the wind in my hair feeling fucking powerful and sexy
